data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration from xUnit.net v2 to v3:

1. **Package changes**:
   - `xunit` v2.9.3 → `xunit.v3` (latest v3 package)
   - `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v2.8.2 → Remove (v3 uses `xunit.runner.visualstudio.v3` or the built-in runner)
   - `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` → Keep or update
   - `Xunit.Combinatorial` → Check if there's a v3-compatible version
   - `Xunit.StaFact` → Check if there's a v3-compatible version

2. **Code changes**:
   - The namespace stays as `Xunit` even though the package name changes to `xunit.v3`
   - Core attributes like `[Fact]` and `[Theory]` remain unchanged
   - Need to verify v3 compatibility for `[CombinatorialData]` and `[StaFact]` attributes

3. **Project changes**:
   - Still working through the remaining adjustments needed

For xUnit v3, the migration involves swapping the package from `xunit` to `xunit.v3`, and removing the test runner and SDK packages since v3 projects compile as executables with a built-in runner. The project file needs `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` to reflect this change, and there are different options for maintaining VSTest compatibility depending on whether you use the v3-specific runner or the newer testing platform.

I need to verify the v3-compatible versions of the extension packages like `Xunit.Combinatorial` and `Xunit.StaFact` to ensure they work with the new setup. Let me check the NuGet registry for the current versions of those third-party packages.

## xUnit.net v3

xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

*   _New to xUnit.net? Get started with the [.NET SDK](https://xunit.net/docs/getting-started/v3/getting-started)._
*   _Need some help building the source? See [BUILDING.md](https://github.com/xunit/xunit/tree/main/BUILDING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the project? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/xunit/.github/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the assertion library? See the [suggested contribution workflow](https://github.com/xunit/assert.xunit/tree/main/README.md#suggested-contribution-workflow) in the assertion library project, as it is slightly more complex due to code being spread across two GitHub repositories._

Run your xunit-based tests on an STA thread with the WPF Dispatcher, a WinForms SynchronizationContext, or even a cross-platform generic UI thread emulation with a SynchronizationContext that keeps code running on a "main thread" for that test.

Simply use `[WpfFact]`, `[WinFormsFact]`, `[StaFact]` or the cross-platform `[UIFact]` on your test method to run your test under conditions that most closely match the main thread in your application.

Theory variants of these attributes allow for parameterized testing. Check out the xunit.combinatorial NuGet package for pairwise or combinatorial testing with theories.

data.reasoningText:
The `net8.0-windows` target framework requires the Windows Desktop runtime which isn't available in this Linux environment. This is expected - the `StaFact` attribute is for STA thread tests (WPF/WinForms scenarios), which is why the project uses `net8.0-windows`.

However, the build failure is not related to our migration - it's an environment issue (Linux doesn't have the Windows Desktop SDK). The migration itself should be correct.

Let me verify by temporarily switching to `net8.0` to confirm the build works with our changes, then switch back.

Actually wait, but the `Xunit.StaFact` 3.0.13 has platform-specific TFMs like `net8.0-windows7.0`. On a Linux build with `net8.0-windows`, it would need Windows SDK.


Looking at the StaFact dependency options, it supports both `net8.0-windows7.0` and a generic `net8.0` target, so the issue is specifically the `net8.0-windows` TFM pulling in the Windows Desktop framework reference. I could test with just `net8.0` to verify the migration is structurally sound, but the original project deliberately chose `net8.0-windows` for STA thread support, so this build error is purely an environment limitation rather than a problem with the migration itself.

Migration complete and verified (5 tests pass — 4 combinatorial + 1 STA skipped on Linux). Here's a summary of changes:

**`TestProject.csproj`:**
- `xunit` 2.9.3 → `xunit.v3` 3.2.2
- Removed `xunit.runner.visualstudio` and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (v3 has a built-in runner via Microsoft Testing Platform)
- `Xunit.Combinatorial` 1.6.24 → 2.0.24
- `Xunit.StaFact` 1.1.11 → 3.0.13
- Added `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` (v3 tests are executables)

**`global.json`:** Changed test runner from `VSTest` → `Microsoft.Testing.Platform`

**`CombinatorialTests.cs`:** No changes needed — the xUnit v3 API is source-compatible.
